feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: RIP Freddy Cannon
entry.content: >Freddy Cannon, a veteran of the early rock scene whose hits of the late ’50s and early ’60s included the top 10 hits “Palisades Park,” “Tallahassee Lassie” and “Way Down Yonder in New Orleans,” died Friday at 89. https://variety.com/2026/music/obit uaries-people-news/freddy-cannon-de ad-palisades-park-rocker-1236815495 /
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: You would've thought Lego would've capitlised on this more with more games durin…
entry.content: You would've thought Lego would've capitlised on this more with more games during the peak. I mostly wanted an open world game in Metru Nui
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /b/ Random: i have the opportunity to go to a medical research facility in the near future, …
entry.content: i have the opportunity to go to a medical research facility in the near future, where i would be staying for up to a year and they would be giving me experimental drugs to see how i would respond to them, they would also be giving me regular meals and a place to sleep...does this sound like a good idea?
entry.dc:creator: /b/ Random

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Do you think Nintendo will ever make another RPG Paper Mario?

entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Why is contemporary children's media so bastardised? Plenty of family films from…
entry.content: Why is contemporary children's media so bastardised? Plenty of family films from 40+ years ago weren't afraid to challenge kids. Are we expected to believe that children of today would reject an authentic, non-comedic, non-sloppified retelling of Animal Farm?
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.content: You ever have that feeling where you're not sure if you're awake or still dreaming?

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Rhythm Heaven Groove
entry.content: Hey, you. No, not Yu. I meant YOU. That's right, (you) over there. You're going to post the medals you've gotten, the perfects you've obtained, and share your rhythmic flow score. Right now. Are we clear? This is a Rhythm Heaven Groove thread, kiddo. Don't fuck around. You don't want to make us angry. And get in that car. Gotta move those wipers.

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Mic use
entry.content: Does anyone use the mic in this game? I bought blops 2 because I skipped the original and I was hoping that people would be yapping in the lobbies like the good old days but I haven't heard a peep so far. Maybe blops 1 has more fun lobbies.
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Gather Me [Neighborhood, 1971] Unlike my straightlaced friends, I've always dug …
entry.content: Gather Me [Neighborhood, 1971] Unlike my straightlaced friends, I've always dug the idea of Melanie--Edith Piaf as Brooklyn waif, preaching the hippie gospel in that absurdly flexible and resonant alto. But I've found the reality cloying. Here she grows up just enough. "Brand New Key" is one of those impossible celebrations of teen libido that renew one's faith in AM radio. "Steppin'" is the best breakup song since "It's Too Late," and though side two slips badly toward the end, she's rarely a s
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Android is so shit now Apple is better
entry.content: As a longtime Samsung user, I bought a iPhone 16 a couple weeks ago and replaced it with my Android and this effectively made me stop doomscrolling on the phone completely. iPhone is so painful to use that it feels like I have a brickphone again for nothing but calling and messaging but I'm not really complaining. Though the iPhone feels better to use and has a better camera and battery and everything else is more or less the same as Android. My biggest complaint is that the keyboard is fucking
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.dc:creator: /b/ Random

entry.title: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says: i realized somethin
entry.content: the human body does not gracefully "shut down" when it is starved of fuel. closest metaphor would be running an NES off of AA batteries. being a home console, it was simply not designed to cope with the slow tapering-off of battery voltage. thus, it will keep running (increasingly corrupted) until the batteries cannot provide any meaningful output at all. now, in my case, i expected... "starvation" to be graceful. like taking a nap, with some tears mixed in. but it has only made me highly unpred
entry.dc:creator: /s4s/ Sh*t 4chan Says

entry.title: /mu/ Music: /mrg/ - Maggie Rogers general
entry.content: Her first two albums, especially The Echo, have a certain innocence to them that would be lost by the time she went commercial on Now That the Light Is Fading. The whole album is a farewell to adolescence and the anxiety of entering adulthood, but what distinguishes it is its strong emotional centerpiece on the closing track "Satellite" which she wrote at age 17. The song is a slow ballad about yearning to be free yet seen that crescendos at its lyrical climax, and damn, do those closing lyrics
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

Could someone listen to an album with songs like mentioned, then change the names afterwards to whatever then listen again and tell me if I’m delusional or whatever.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Mike Love lounged back on the couch, his muscular, tattooed body on full display…
entry.content: Mike Love lounged back on the couch, his muscular, tattooed body on full display: ripped abs, powerful thighs, and that perfect, tight ass. "Come on, Brian. You know you want to taste me." Brian Wilson, washed-up and pathetic with his unkempt hair and flabby body, knelt between Mike's spread legs. Tears already streamed down his ugly face as he pressed his mouth to Mike's asshole, out of fear that Mike would beat and rape him. He licked tentatively at first, then deeper, tongue swirling around t
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Affordable housing
entry.content: >Can one bill make housing more affordable? No. You can't legislate that. You need new technology to do that. That's our job /g/ Let's discuss technology that can make houses more affordable. https://www.npr.org/2026/06/28/nx-s 1-5869726/can-one-bill-make-housing -more-affordable

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Why don't normies care about mass surveillance? It feels so weird now that anyth…
entry.content: Why don't normies care about mass surveillance? It feels so weird now that anything you do is put in a database and stored in some cloud. it feels so invasive and gross. it doesn't feel like that long ago you could go into town and go somewhere and no one knew except you and the cashier. Maybe you'd be on some cctv grainy VHS tape but that's it.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Hello /pol/, my catalytic converter was removed and reinstalled to enable replac…
entry.content: Hello /pol/, my catalytic converter was removed and reinstalled to enable replacing another part. Since then I've gotten the CEL P2099 Post-Catalyst Fuel Trim System Too Rich (Bank 2). There's obviously a 99% chance that it's correlated. I'm a wrenchlet so I explained this to a mechanic, and they said to replace the post cat O2 sensor. Would that actually fix it, or is there a definitive way to know if it's something else, like an exhaust leak from the cat not being secured back on properly? Or
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /mu/ Music: 'bum on tum
entry.content: Thought I'd give this another shot, just less frequently Today's listen: The Viscounts - Harlem Nocturne The Viscounts were a New Jersey instrumental band who secured their status as a footnote in pop history by charting twice with the same song. Their version of "Harlem Nocturne" reached the Hot 100 in 1959, and the Top 40 in 1966. The recording is moody and atmospheric, like a sleazier cousin to the Peter Gunn theme. The bass prowls, the guitar spreads like fog, and the sax is downright lasciv
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: After a year and a half, I have finished the Kiryu Saga. Played them in between …
entry.content: After a year and a half, I have finished the Kiryu Saga. Played them in between other things. My rank, in order: >0 >5 >1 >6 >4 >2 >3 Already had played Judgement and its sequel, and 7 because it can be played alone. Can't wait for Stranger Than Heaven. Guess I'll play 8 or Gaiden next. I like playing jumping around.
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /o/ Auto: When welding in new sheet metal at certain parts of the car the German inspectio…
entry.content: When welding in new sheet metal at certain parts of the car the German inspection authorities demand the following: 1) There has to be an overlap (no butt welding) 2) Spot welding (no continuous seams) How the fuck do I protect my car against future rust issues then? Water will get in at the points where there is no seam and it will sit right inbetween the 2 sheets which overlap. A Weld-Thru Primer won't do the trick, will it? Maybe delay the issue a bit. What about using body solder? There is a
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Pillar of the Western Civilization
entry.content: >Iliad >Trojan War starts because a whore (Helen) and 2 simps (Menelaus and Paris) >Odyssey >Achaean version of "Moses' 40 years in the dessert", but in the sea >only Odysseus survives, forget about his men (brothers in Ares) >he's now a pathetic man Perhaps the Western Civilization deserves to fall for being based in simpism and female-coded drama. Christopher Nolan is a based accelerationist, he is the Avatar of the 13th Olympian God, Momus the 'Exiled One'.
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /b/ Random: >on the intercom Rosie tells me to come >she didn’t have a daughter but she did …
entry.content: >on the intercom Rosie tells me to come >she didn’t have a daughter but she did have a son >"the lift doesn’t work, run up the stairs and come" >"if you don’t quick you are not gonna know your son" >I grab a bunch of roses and started to run >two months later >"come and get your son" >"I don’t want you baby to come and tie me down" >"you are old, and I am young" >"while I am young I wanna have some fun"

entry.title: /int/ International: How does your populace view your military as well as veterans?
entry.content: I can sympathize with veterans and acknowledge the role that the military has played in providing careers and uprooting many from poverty. However I find the weak morality of the US government to be a source of shame to the country. Why should so many be sacrificed in chess game that those who are most impacted did not consent to play?
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /flg/ - F-List General #8376 Cock Fucking Edition Previous Thread: >>84147911 F-…
entry.content: /flg/ - F-List General #8376 Cock Fucking Edition Previous Thread: >>84147911 F-list is a website for people interested in erotic roleplaying, and creating character profiles to find like-minded partners with similar interests and fetishes to roleplay with. It features a webchat (which can be used for textual roleplaying), group forums and more kinks and depravity than you can memorize. The purpose of this thread is to serve as a place where roleplayers can freely share and advertise their profi
entry.dc:creator: /trash/ Off-Topic

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: My uncle died.
entry.content: My uncle was an English sailor, he sailed all across the caribbean coast and seeded his wild oats. died and he bequeathed me his possessions. One of them was an interesting book. Might I dare the devil and open it?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Github star history
entry.content: Why did Github remove star history? Now you cannot view the history of how a repository gained its stars unless you are the owner or a collaborator. Right now there are so many AI slop projects with tens of thousands of fake stars. Is Github trying to keep this a secret? I do not understand this. For example: https://github.com/ruvnet/RuView Totally fake project. 81k stars (obviously fake stars). But it doesn't work at all. What's the point?
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Frodo, the Dark Lord's eyes are upon Lothlórien. Do you think I look fat in thi…
entry.content: >Frodo, the Dark Lord's eyes are upon Lothlórien. Do you think I look fat in this dress? I don't want Hal-, I mean, Sauron to think that he dodged an arrowand forced me to downgrade. I mean, Celeborn is wise and all, but the guy can't read the fucking room for the life of him. You saw how he was going on and on about much desiring to speak with Gandalf when the dude was clearly dead and you mortal pussies were obviously crying over him? Everyone was so embarrassed they didn't even want to answer
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Half-Life 2: Episode One
entry.content: What are your thoughts on Half-Life 2: Episode One? I believe it is mildly underappreciated. Sure, it doesn't have as much to chew on as the base game or Episode Two, but the things it does add are great; additionally, the combat sections were memorable, especially the dark areas where having to conserve your suit energy meant life or death.
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games
